title: Sony limits purchase of disc drives and reignites debate on the future of physical games
summary: Sony imposes limits on the purchase of PS5 disc readers as the industry questions the end of physical media and gamers defend game ownership.

title: Google sparks controversy with ad imagining founding fathers drafting Declaration of Independence with AI
summary: Google's commercial shows historical figures using artificial intelligence to draft the Declaration of Independence, drawing criticism for trivializing AI.

title: Microsoft allows disabling Copilot, Facilitator, and Recap in Teams after user complaints
summary: The company responds to criticism about forced AI integration in Teams and offers options to disable them during meetings.

title: CIA director equates advanced AI with nuclear weapons
summary: The CIA's top official warns that artificial intelligence poses a risk comparable to nuclear weapons for global security.

title: SpaceX vaporizes 260 Starlink satellites in six months using Earth's atmosphere, raising environmental concerns
summary: Burning large satellites in the atmosphere triggers ecological alerts as FCC seeks to exempt them from regulations.

title: Germany's largest video game preservation project collapses after running out of funds
summary: The archive of 60,000 games, which was completely public, is abandoned after losing 1.5 million euros in funding.

title: China-backed AI tool that created a fake Brad Pitt fight makes its way into Hollywood
summary: Chinese deepfake technology begins to be used in the film industry, raising concerns about misinformation.

title: AI browsers can be hypnotized to attack their users
summary: Researchers discover that it is possible to manipulate smart browsers to execute malicious actions against their own owners.

title: Data center emits constant screeching noise directly affecting a man's home
summary: A resident denounces the unbearable noise generated by a nearby data center facility, raising coexistence issues.

title: U.S. Navy tests 3D-printed fighter jet parts that cut repair times in half
summary: Components manufactured with 3D printing will be installed on operational F/A-18 Super Hornets to speed up maintenance.

title: New synthetic graft material eliminates bone cancer and regenerates bone
summary: The innovative material demonstrated the ability to destroy cancer cells and promote the growth of new bone tissue.

title: Jim Keller's startup builds factory to mass-produce small semiconductor plants
summary: Atomic Semi rebrands as Fab2 and plans to revolutionize chip manufacturing with compact, modular factories.

title: Microsoft collaborated with FBI to identify 19-year-old hacker using unique Windows 11 code
summary: The use of an operating system identifier allowed tracking and arresting a young person linked to the Scattered Spider extortion group.

title: Alibaba bans use of Anthropic's Claude Code after discovering alleged China-detection backdoor
summary: The dispute between the companies intensifies: Alibaba orders its employees to migrate to its own tool Qoder.

title: EU Council fast-tracks controversial chat control measure
summary: The new regulation will allow scanning of private communications, drawing strong criticism from privacy advocates.

title: Private AI schools offer personalized learning to wealthy families in the US
summary: These educational centers use artificial intelligence to adapt teaching to each student, attracting parents seeking alternatives to traditional education.

title: World's most powerful solar installation is in the Pyrenees and reaches 3,500 °C
summary: The Odeillo solar furnace, in the Pyrenees, is capable of generating extreme temperatures for research, without being a power plant.

title: Fossilized feces reveals that Asia's driest desert housed a forest 4,000 years ago
summary: Analysis of coprolites shows that the current Taklamakan Desert in China was a lush forest in ancient times.

title: They propose a giant 400-ton space airbag to protect Earth from solar superstorms
summary: Scientists suggest deploying a large-scale magnetic shield to deflect harmful solar particles.

title: Brussels warns that two-thirds of cinnamon sold in Europe has quality issues or is fraudulent
summary: A European Union report reveals that most cinnamon on the market contains adulterations or fails to meet standards.
